title: calophyllum coriaceum
intro: Calophyllum coriaceum, also known as the keruing tree or Burmese lacquer tree, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its smooth, grey bark and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calophyllum coriaceum include the potential treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory disorders, as well as the prevention of scurvy and other diseases related to vitamin C deficiency. Calophyllum coriaceum can be prepared into herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
